attributes.title: Kala Pharmaceuticals to sell 1.197M shares at $5.85 in private placement
attributes.content:
• KALA BIO (NASDAQ:KALA) has entered into a securities purchase agreement with a select group of institutional accredited investors for the sale, in a private placement, of shares of its common stock and shares of its Series H Convertible Non-Redeemable Preferred Stock, for aggregate gross proceeds of ~$12.5M.
  • In the private placement, KALA has agreed to sell 1.2M shares of its common stock at a price of $5.85/share and 9,393 shares of its Series H Preferred Stock at a price of $585/share.
  • KALA intends to use the net proceeds from the private placement to advance the clinical development of KPI-012 for the treatment of persistent corneal epithelial defect, as well as for general corporate purposes.
  • The private placement is expected to close on or about June 28, 2024.
  • KALA +27.83% premarket to $5.87.

attributes.title: Arista Networks rises as Citi boosts price target, citing rising AI opportunity
attributes.content:
• Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) was in focus on Thursday as Citi upped its price target, citing a rising opportunity in artificial intelligence.
  • Shares rose fractionally in premarket trading
  • Though InfiniBand is thought to take the majority of the AI networking market, Ethernet is clearly gaining share as well and the AI back-end switching market total addressable market could reach $15B by 2027, up from $10B, analyst Atif Malik said. Malik boosted his price target on Arista to $385 from $330 and maintained his Buy rating.
  • Malik also raised his earnings estimates for 2024, 2025 and 2026 by 4%, 16% and 13%, respectively.

More on Arista

attributes.content:
• Oragenics (NYSE:OGEN) on Thursday said that it had completed the development of its prototype of its automated intranasal device that would help in treating concussed patients.
  • The company added that a phase II study is being designed to analyze the effectiveness of ONP-002 on blood biomarkers and patient-reported outcomes of concussed patients.
  • The breath-propelled device requires the patient to administer the drug into the nose.
  • Press release.

attributes.title: Boeing is said to see sharp decline in fuselage defects from Spirit AeroSystems
attributes.content:
• Boeing (NYSE:BA) saw a sharp drop in defects on 737 Max fuselages arriving from supplier Spirit AeroSystems Holdings (SPR) and a faster pace for the narrowbody planes through the assembly line at its factory near Seattle, the company said during a two-day tour of facilities for reporters.

  • Boeing (BA) this year has faced greater scrutiny from aviation authorities and lawmakers following a midflight blowout of a door panel on a 737 Max 9 operating by Alaska Airlines (ALK) in January. The aviation giant has taken steps to revamp its safety procedures with the aim of increasing plane output eventually.

  • Boeing (BA) has said it wants to acquire Spirit AeroSystems (SPR) to regain control of a key part of its supply chain.

attributes.title: TotalEnergies to sell non-core U.K. upstream assets
attributes.content:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) said Thursday it agreed to sell its West of Shetland oil and gas fields in the U.K. to privately-held Prax Group for an undisclosed sum.

The French company is selling its entire interest in West of Shetland fields - Laggan, Tormore, Glenlivet, Edradour and Glendronach - as well as the Shetland gas plant and nearby exploration licenses; the mature assets currently produce ~7,500 boe/day (~90% gas) net to the company.

TotalEnergies (TTE) said it remains committed to the U.K. through its upstream portfolio in the North Sea and its integrated power and renewables portfolio.

Separately, TotalEnergies (TTE) said it agreed to acquire a 60% interest and operatorship in Block STP02 offshore Sao Tome and Principe for an undisclosed sum.

More on TotalEnergies

  • TotalEnergies: Could Namibia Be Its Guyana Moment?
  • TotalEnergies: The Virtue Of Consistency With 2 New Upsides

attributes.title: Pixelworks plans to reduce workforce by ~16%
attributes.content:
• Semiconductor and software solutions company Pixelworks (NASDAQ:PXLW) said on Thursday that its board has approved a restructuring plan, which is expected to result in ~16% reduction in the workforce.
  • PXLW shares were trading 0.80% lower pre-market.
  • The layoffs are estimated to take place in operations, research and development, sales, marketing and administration.
  • The reduction will see restructuring charges of ~$1.7M related to employee severance and benefits, largely to be recorded in Q2 ending June 30.
  • The company said the move will more appropriately align its operating expenses with current revenue levels.
  • The restructuring, set to close by the end of the current quarter, will result in annual savings of ~$4M to PXLW.

attributes.title: Vanda Pharmaceuticals drops after Future Pak walks away
attributes.content:
Vanda Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VNDA) fell 5.3% in premarket trading after Future Pak said it's withdrawing its offer for the company.

"This action comes after a continued lack of engagement from Vanda and the receipt of Vanda’s rejection of Future Pak’s final proposal," Future Pak said in a statement on Wednesday.

A week ago Vanda (VNDA) rejected two unsolicited buyout proposals by Cycle Group Holdings and Future Pak, LLC, citing concerns over valuation. The decision came after contract manufacturer Future Pak revised the cash portion of its most recent takeover bid to $8.50–$9.00 from $7.25 to $7.75 per share earlier this month.

attributes.title: Vivos gains as pilot program validates new market model
attributes.content:
Vivos Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VVOS) traded higher in the premarket on Thursday after announcing that a pilot program involving multiple medical facilities validated the assumptions behind its newly launched marketing and distribution model.

The developer of Vivos CARE medical devices for obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) conducted the analysis using data from patients who visited seven dental offices and one ENT specialist office across several states over seven months.

The company said that out of 99 patients seen by the providers under the program, 79% of newly diagnosed adult OSA patients preferred its oral appliance therapy over continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) machines or no treatment options.

According to Vivos (VVOS), the results validated the newly launched model, through which the company intends to yield revenue by partnering with dentists and sleep therapy providers to make patients aware of OSA treatment options.

More on Vivos Therapeutics

attributes.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures tick down after Micron's outlook disappoints
attributes.content:
Stock index futures slipped on Thursday after Micron Technology's guidance failed to impress investors, and markets received a slew of economic data.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 2 basis points to 4.31%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) slid 2 basis points to 4.73%. See how Treasury yields have done across the curve at the Seeking Alpha bond page

The major market averages advanced a bit after seesawing through most of Wednesday, as stocks struggled for momentum.

"Markets have struggled over the last 24 hours, with sovereign bonds selling off and equities losing ground for the most part," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"To be fair, it wasn’t all bad news, and a renewed tech advance did see the Magnificent 7 reach a new all-time high," Allen added.

Micron Technology (MU) was down 5% in premarket trading after the memory chipmaker issued guidance that was in-line with estimates, overshadowing a stronger-than-expected third-quarter.

Markets are also bracing for the personal-consumption expenditures data due on Friday. Core PCE is the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge.

On the economic front, U.S. gross domestic product in the U.S. Department of Commerce's final Q1 estimate rose at an annual rate of 1.4%, slightly higher than the second estimate of 1.3%, but lower than Q4 2023's strong print of 3.4%.

Jobless claims declined for the second month as claims fell -6K to 233K. At the same time, retail inventories moved up 0.7% in May, and the U.S. international trade deficit widened more than expected in May.

Durable goods orders also edged up in May. Durable goods orders rose 0.1% M/M in May, which was better than what was expected by forecasters.

The Fed balance sheet will also be released later in the day.

Political action will also keep investors busy today, with the first presidential debate between President Joe Biden and former President Donald Trump on the way.

"Investors tend to regard Biden’s policies as representing some continuity, and to dismiss Trump’s policies as campaign rhetoric that would not actually be implemented," Donovan added.

More on markets

  • You Can Ring My Bell
  • What Market Breadth Tells Us About The Current Rally

attributes.title: Annovis Bio files patent for new manufacturing process of buntanetap
attributes.content:
• Annovis Bio (NYSE:ANVS) Thursday announced the filing of a provisional patent covering the manufacturing process of new solid forms of buntanetap.
  • This patent encompasses the methods for manufacturing the new crystalline form of buntanetap, which has demonstrated exceptional stability and efficacy.
  • The patent also covers the entire synthesis process, from basic starting materials to finished (>99.9% pure) GMP product, suitable for manufacturing scale at ton quantities, Annovis Bio (ANVS) said.
  • The CMC protocol was submitted to the FDA and will be discussed in July, with the goal of continuing development of our drug and filing the NDA with the new crystal form.
  • ANVS +0.35% premarket to $5.75.
  • Source: Press Release

attributes.title: Chewy buys back shares as the free cash flow story takes shape
attributes.content:
Chewy (NYSE:CHWY) is on watch after striking an agreement to repurchase $500 million worth of equity directly from its BC Partners. The transaction is fully incremental to the company's recently implemented $500M share buyback program, with initial expectations to be active in the market immediately.

Mizuho Securities analyst David Bellinger thinks the development is another key indicator that free cash flow generation is beginning to inflect for Chewy (CHWY) and allowing for newfound capital returns. He also thinks that some of the expected catalysts for Chewy (CHWY) are beginning to materialize. "The next and most consequential factor, in our view, is a clear and sustainable return to new customer growth," he highlighted.

Shares of Chewy (CHWY) were down 1.51% in premarket trading on Thursday, after shedding 2.83% during the Wednesday session. However, the online retail stock had been on a hot streak since Q1 earnings were reported on May 29. With the earnings release, Chewy (CHWY) disclosed that sales were up 3.1% during Q1 to $2.88 billion, and adjusted EBITDA rose 47% from last year's mark to $162.9 million. Gross margin improved during the quarter to 28.2% of sales from 28.1% a year ago. Net income for the quarter was $66.9 million, up from $22.9 million a year ago.

attributes.title: U.S. GDP growth revised up slightly to 1.4% in final Q1 estimate
attributes.content:
U.S. real gross domestic product in the U.S. Department of Commerce's final Q1 estimate rose at an annual rate of 1.4%, slightly higher than the second estimate of 1.3%, but lower than Q4 2023's strong print of 3.4%.

The upward revision mainly reflected a downward revision to imports and upward revisions to nonresidential fixed investment and government spending, the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis said on Thursday. These revisions were partly offset by a downward revision to consumer spending.

Specifically, retail trade, construction, and finance and insurance were the biggest contributors to the latest GDP reading.

Real disposable income was revised down to +1.3% from +1.9%. The personal savings rate of 3.8% was unchanged from the previous estimate.

PCE price index: +3.4% vs. 3.3% second estimate and +1.8% in Q4 2023. Excluding food and energy, core PCE price index stood at +3.7%, up from +3.6% in the second estimate and +2.0% in the prior quarter.
